t: Adding a Chapter to a Playground Book
a: Create a folder with a manifest that describes the chapter’s name and page order.

t: Adding a Cutscene to a Playground Book
a: Create a subfolder, a manifest file, and cutscene metadata.

t: Adding a Glossary to a Playground Book
a: Define terms in the glossary property list and reference them on playground pages.

t: Adding a Page to a Playground Book
a: Create a subfolder, a manifest file, and a content file.

t: Adding a Swift package to your app playground
a: Extend the functionality of your app playground by finding and adding a publicly available Swift package.

t: Creating a subscription
a: Create a machine-readable summary of a set of playground books to make them available for others to download.

t: Creating and Running a Playground Book
a: Build a playground book from a template, and run it in Swift Playgrounds.

t: Customizing the Completions in the Shortcut Bar
a: Guide learners toward a solution by hiding some symbols and showing others.

t: Debugging an App Playground using the Console
a: Learn different methods to debug your code using console output.

t: Giving hints to help learners solve problems
a: Add hints, spoilers, and solutions to a page to help teach the material.

t: Hiding Code from a Playground Page
a: Use special Swift comments to hide code from display but continue to run it.

t: Importing sample content into user app playgrounds
a: Learn how to bring sample code into your app playground.

t: Localizing Code Comments and String Literals
a: In Swift Playgrounds 3.0 and later, mark up code zones to replace them with code that’s localized for the current user.

t: Localizing a Subscription Feed
a: Provide multiple localizations of a subscription’s content in a single feed.

t: Playground Books
a: Create interactive Playground Book based learning experiences for the Swift Playgrounds app.

t: Previewing SwiftUI views in Swift Playgrounds
a: Use the canvas in Swift Playgrounds to see a live preview of the SwiftUI views in your app.

t: Publishing a Subscription
a: Make the playground books in a series available online for download and subscription.

t: Requesting access to capabilities for your app playground
a: Request access for your app to protected resources, services, or device hardware such as sensors.

t: Sharing Resources in a Playground Book
a: Reuse common assets throughout a book to reduce its size.

t: Specifying Editable Regions in a Playground Page
a: Guide learning by marking code that learners can change or copy forward.

t: Structuring Content for Swift Playgrounds
a: Add content to a playground book by creating new folders and property lists.

t: Swift Playgrounds
a: Learn to write apps, repurpose sample code, create, and share learning experiences in Swift Playgrounds.

t: Using Modules to Share Code in a Playground Book
a: In Swift Playgrounds 3.0 and later, make code available across multiple chapters to teach the value of reusable code.

t: Writing Prose for a Playground Page
a: Add comment markers in your Swift code to mark text as prose.
